text: Last summer, I finally planned a trip to Japan after saving money for months. I spent weeks researching flights, hotels, and must-see attractions. On my first day, I tried ramen from a small street stall, and the rich broth surprised me. Exploring the quiet temples and busy markets, I quickly noticed how different the culture was from my own—politeness and order were everywhere. Language barriers were tricky; I used hand gestures and a translation app to order food or ask for directions. Despite the confusion, I created memories that will last forever. Traveling taught me that stepping into the unknown is always worth it.
